title: SSRF Defense, Prototype-Safe Data, Terminal Injection, Paths, and Resource Limits · Type Recall
summary: A delayed maintainer review reconstructs ssrf defense, prototype-safe data, terminal injection, paths, and resource limits from revision, runtime, command, unknown input, validation result, request identity, response fixture, promise state, cache decision, output, abort, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for deny-by-default TypeScript trust-boundary policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Revalidate scheme, hostname, port, path class, and redirect count for every destination and reject user-controlled egress.
Use own-property checks, explicit field construction, safe records, and no blind assignment from unknown objects.
Normalize or visibly escape control, ANSI, bidi, and non-printing sequences from commands, API payloads, and files.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex Ts Redirect Policy · changed case
steps.instruction:
review case — A review TypeScript team handles case 1252 during an inclusive museum learning kiosk. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to enforce redirect, prototype, terminal, path, privacy, and resource boundaries; the release claim must bind source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, and packed artifact; then change one compiler, Node runtime, emitted artifact, package, migration, rollback, or restore condition and reconcile evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Revalidate scheme, hostname, port, path class, and redirect count for every destination and reject user-controlled egress.
